artifact_description: A single factual sentence defining Go programming language for quick technical reference publication
content: Go is an open-source, compiled, statically typed, garbage-collected programming language that emphasizes simplicity, readability, and fast compilation while providing built-in support for high-performance concurrent programming through lightweight goroutines and channels for building simple, reliable, and efficient software across multiple platforms.
summary: Single-sentence technical definition of Go verifying core characteristics: compiled, statically typed, garbage-collected, goroutine-based concurrency, cross-platform
